check_proof.py
---- MODULE PaxosProof_PigeonHole ----
EXTENDS Integers, NaturalsInduction, TLAPS
(* ---- Content from module Sets ---- *)
  \** NB: Module NaturalsInduction comes from the TLAPS library, usually
  \** installed in /usr/local/lib/tlaps. Make sure this is in your Toolbox
  \** search path, see Preferences/TLA+ Preferences.

IsBijection(f, S, T) == /\ f \in [S -> T]
                        /\ \A x, y \in S : (x # y) => (f[x] # f[y])
                        /\ \A y \in T : \E x \in S : f[x] = y


IsFiniteSet(S) == \E n \in Nat : \E f : IsBijection(f, 1..n, S)

(****************************************************************************)
(* Finite sets and cardinality are defined in the TLA+ standard module      *)
(* FiniteSets, but this is not yet natively supported by TLAPS. For the     *)
(* time being, we use the following axiom for defining set cardinality.     *)
(****************************************************************************)
\* Cardinality(S) == CHOOSE n : (n \in Nat) /\ \E f : IsBijection(f, 1..n, S)

CONSTANT Cardinality(_)
AXIOM CardinalityAxiom ==
         \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) =>
           \A n : (n = Cardinality(S)) <=>
                    (n \in Nat) /\ \E f : IsBijection(f, 1..n, S)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityInNat == \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) => Cardinality(S) \in Nat
  PROOF OMITTED

------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityZero ==
           /\ IsFiniteSet({})
           /\ Cardinality({}) = 0
           /\ \A S : IsFiniteSet(S) /\ (Cardinality(S)=0) => (S = {})
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM CardinalityPlusOne ==
    ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
           NEW x, x \notin S
    PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cup {x})
           /\ Cardinality(S \cup {x}) = Cardinality(S) + 1
  PROOF OMITTED

------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityOne == \A m : /\ IsFiniteSet({m})
                                 /\ Cardinality({m}) = 1
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM CardinalityTwo == \A m, p : m # p => 
                              /\ IsFiniteSet({m,p})
                              /\ Cardinality({m,p}) = 2
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM IntervalCardinality ==  
  ASSUME NEW a \in Nat, NEW b \in Nat 
  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(a..b)
         /\ Cardinality(a..b) = IF a > b THEN 0 ELSE b-a+1
  PROOF OMITTED

------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityOneConverse ==
   ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S), Cardinality(S) = 1
   PROVE  \E m : S = {m}
  PROOF OMITTED

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM IsBijectionInverse ==
  ASSUME NEW f, NEW S, NEW T, 
         IsBijection(f, S, T) 
  PROVE  \E g : IsBijection(g, T, S)
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM IsBijectionTransitive ==
  ASSUME NEW f1, NEW f2, NEW S, NEW T, NEW U, 
           IsBijection(f1, S, U),
           IsBijection(f2, U, T) 
  PROVE  \E g : IsBijection(g, S, T)
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM IsBijectionCardinality ==
  \A f, S, T : /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
               /\ IsFiniteSet(T)
               => (IsBijection(f, S, T) <=> Cardinality(S) = Cardinality(T))

LEMMA CardinalitySetMinus ==
      ASSUME NEW S, IsFiniteSet(S),
             NEW x \in S
      PROVE /\ IsFiniteSet(S \ {x})
            /\ Cardinality(S \ {x}) = Cardinality(S) - 1
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM FiniteSubset ==
  ASSUME NEW S, NEW TT, IsFiniteSet(TT), S \subseteq TT
  PROVE  /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
         /\ Cardinality(S) \leq Cardinality(TT)
  PROOF OMITTED

-------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM CardinalityUnion ==
          \A S, T : IsFiniteSet(S) /\ IsFiniteSet(T) =>
                      /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cup T)
                      /\ IsFiniteSet(S \cap T)
                      /\ Cardinality(S \cup T) =
                              Cardinality(S) + Cardinality(T)
                              - Cardinality(S \cap T)  

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM PigeonHole ==
            \A S, T : /\ IsFiniteSet(S)
                      /\ IsFiniteSet(T)
                      /\ Cardinality(T) < Cardinality(S)
                      => \A f \in [S -> T] :
                           \E x, y \in S : x # y /\ f[x] = f[y]
PROOF OBVIOUS

========================================

item.text: I’ll replace the placeholder with a contradiction proof: assume injectivity, derive a bijection onto `T`, then contradict the strict cardinality inequality.
That tiny patch was only a probe and won’t be sufficient. I’m replacing it with a full structured proof now.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "cat > /tmp/newproof.txt <<'EOF'
PROOF
  <1>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW S, NEW T,
                       HfinS == IsFiniteSet(S),
                       HfinT == IsFiniteSet(T),
                       Hlt == Cardinality(T) < Cardinality(S),
                       NEW f \\in [S -> T]
                PROVE  \\E x, y \\in S : x # y /\\ f[x] = f[y]
    OBVIOUS
  <1>2. LET R == { f[x] : x \\in S }
        IN  <2>1. R \\subseteq T
              BY <1>1 DEF R
            <2>2. IsFiniteSet(R)
              BY <1>1, <2>1, FiniteSubset
            <2>3. Cardinality(R) \\leq Cardinality(T)
              BY <1>1, <2>1, FiniteSubset
            <2>4. SUFFICES ASSUME Hinj == \\A x, y \\in S : (x # y) => (f[x] # f[y])
                           PROVE  FALSE
              BY <1>1
            <2>5. IsBijection(f, S, R)
              BY <1>1, <2>4 DEF IsBijection, R
            <2>6. Cardinality(S) = Cardinality(R)
              BY <1>1, <2>2, <2>5, IsBijectionCardinality
            <2>7. FALSE
              BY <1>1, <2>3, <2>6
            <2>8. QED
              BY <2>4, <2>7
  <1>3. QED
    BY <1>2
EOF
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('PaxosProof_PigeonHole.tla')
s=p.read_text()
start=s.index('PROOF\\n  BY DEF IsBijection')
end=s.index('\\n\\n========================================', start)
new=Path('/tmp/newproof.txt').read_text().rstrip()
p.write_text(s[:start]+new+s[end:])
PY
sed -n '116,170p' PaxosProof_PigeonHole.tla"

item.text: The parser also dislikes a proof block inside `LET`. I’ll restructure with explicit `DEFINE` so the range set is available to steps.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "cat > /tmp/newproof.txt <<'EOF'
PROOF
  <1>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW S, NEW T,
                       IsFiniteSet(S),
                       IsFiniteSet(T),
                       Cardinality(T) < Cardinality(S),
                       NEW f \\in [S -> T]
                PROVE  \\E x, y \\in S : x # y /\\ f[x] = f[y]
    OBVIOUS
  <1>2. DEFINE R == { f[x] : x \\in S }
  <1>3. R \\subseteq T
    BY <1>1 DEF R
  <1>4. IsFiniteSet(R)
    BY <1>1, <1>3, FiniteSubset
  <1>5. Cardinality(R) \\leq Cardinality(T)
    BY <1>1, <1>3, FiniteSubset
  <1>6. SUFFICES ASSUME \\A x, y \\in S : (x # y) => (f[x] # f[y])
                 PROVE  FALSE
    BY <1>1
  <1>7. IsBijection(f, S, R)
    BY <1>1, <1>6 DEF IsBijection, R
  <1>8. Cardinality(S) = Cardinality(R)
    BY <1>1, <1>4, <1>7, IsBijectionCardinality
  <1>9. FALSE
    BY <1>1, <1>5, <1>8
  <1>10. QED
    BY <1>6, <1>9
EOF
python3 - <<'PY'

p=Path('PaxosProof_PigeonHole.tla')
s=p.read_text()
start=s.index('PROOF\\n', s.index('THEOREM PigeonHole'))
end=s.index('\\n\\n========================================', start)
p.write_text(s[:start]+Path('/tmp/newproof.txt').read_text().rstrip()+s[end:])
PY

(* Each of these pragmas can be cited with a BY or a USE.  The pragma that *)

(* This method translates the proof obligation to SMTLIB2. The supported  *)

(**************************************************************************)
